level_signals: 7+ years of product management experience on developer tools
Owns the full Postgres surface across five engineering teams
Responsible for Supabase's biggest infrastructure bet (Multigres)

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 7+ years of product management experience on developer tools, or an ex-founder with strong product instincts.
You're technical enough to read an architecture doc, follow a design discussion, and ask the right questions.
Have shipped products where the database behaviour is the experience. You know how connection limits, query planning, and extension behaviour translate to customer pain.
Are biased toward speed. You'd rather ship something imperfect and learn than spend another sprint refining a spec. You know when to stop planning and start building.
Build with what you own. You use the products on your roadmap: running queries, testing the connection pooler, prototyping against the Data API. You develop opinions through first-hand use.
Use AI to move faster. You use AI tools to compress the slow parts of the job (research, drafting, synthesising feedback) so you spend more time on judgment calls.
Work async by default. Supabase is fully remote. You make decisions in writing and move work forward without meetings.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Familiarity with the Postgres ecosystem (extensions, tooling, community) is a bonus.

scope.surface: Full Postgres surface: Multigres, OrioleDB, Postgres observability/extensions/security, connection pooler, Data API
core_responsibilities: Talk to developers across the full spectrum (startups, enterprises, engineers debugging production issues)
Lead Multigres to production adoption — define migration path, rollout story, and success metrics
Own problem definition, requirements, and success metrics for every capability shipped
Prioritise across competing surfaces: platform reliability, developer-experience, enterprise requirements
Keep engineering, design, and leadership aligned on the roadmap across five engineering teams

green_flags: Fully remote with global hiring — no location restrictions
Clear product surface and ownership area (Multigres + full Postgres stack)
ESOP equity offered to all team members
100% health insurance coverage for employees, 80% for dependents
Company is well-funded ($500M raised) with large developer community (7M+ developers)
Open-source-first culture with transparent, build-in-public ethos
Annual education and tech allowances provided
red_flags: Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for non-US candidates despite global hiring claim
7+ years PM requirement is a hard floor with very limited bridging path (only exception is ex-founder)
Role scope is extremely broad (five engineering teams, multiple critical surfaces) — could indicate under-resourcing

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required + team scope; not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and open-source emphasis)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly listed as metrics)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'We hire globally' and 'no Supabase offices')
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred as false from global hiring policy; not explicitly stated)
ambiguities: The 7+ years requirement reads 'or an ex-founder with strong product instincts' — it is unclear whether ex-founder status fully substitutes for the years requirement or is an additional qualifier. Treated as an alternative pathway rather than a separate hard requirement.
Visa sponsorship is never mentioned; 'We hire globally' implies openness but does not confirm sponsorship. Marked as 'unclear'.

Severity of 'shipped products where the database behaviour is the experience' — placed under 'You might be a good fit if' which is softer language, but the adjacent sentence about knowing connection limits/query planning reads as an expectation. Treated as hard given the centrality of the role.
